meta.architecture_style: Full-stack Next.js monolith with MVC-like layering
meta.executive_summary: PinLaunch is a Next.js 15 full-stack web app that generates landing pages from GitHub repos using Claude or Gemini AI. Users collect inspiration pins, select style presets, connect a GitHub repo, and stream AI-generated HTML/CSS files in real-time via SSE. The stack is React 19 frontend, Next.js API routes as backend, SQLite via better-sqlite3 for persistence, and Anthropic/Google AI SDKs for generation. Architecture follows App Router conventions with components in src/components, API handlers in src/app/api, and shared business logic in src/lib.

file_placement_rules.description: Each feature gets its own directory with route.ts
Parameterized routes use Next.js bracket conventions
All UI components live flat in src/components
Shared backend logic and DB access go in src/lib
Tests co-located with implementation in src/lib
App Router pages and layouts only in src/app

naming_conventions.description: Next.js App Router requires route.ts for API handlers
Feature name + role suffix (Panel, Modal, Card, Frame)
Co-located test files with .test.ts or .integration.test.ts suffix

architectural_style.title: Full-stack Next.js monolith
architectural_style.chosen: Single Next.js app serving both frontend and backend API routes
architectural_style.rationale: Eliminates cross-service complexity; API routes share TypeScript types with frontend; simpler deployment

key_decisions.title: SQLite over managed database
key_decisions.chosen: better-sqlite3 with local file at data/builder.db
key_decisions.rationale: Zero-config persistence for single-user local tool; WAL mode handles concurrency

key_decisions.title: Dual AI provider support
key_decisions.chosen: Runtime switching between Claude and Gemini via settings table
key_decisions.rationale: Users can use whichever API key they have; Claude for streaming tool-use, Gemini as fallback

key_decisions.title: SSE over WebSockets for streaming
key_decisions.chosen: Server-Sent Events via ReadableStream in Next.js API routes
key_decisions.rationale: Unidirectional stream fits generation use case; no WebSocket server needed; works on Vercel

key_decisions.title: No external state management library
key_decisions.chosen: React useState/useEffect with sessionStorage for persistence
key_decisions.rationale: App has linear state flow; no cross-component sharing complex enough to warrant Redux/Zustand

key_decisions.title: File-system output for generated sites
key_decisions.chosen: Generated files written to process.cwd()/output/ directory
key_decisions.rationale: Simple local preview via /api/preview catchall route; no cloud storage needed

trade_offs.accept: SQLite on local filesystem resets on Vercel redeploy
Types duplicated between frontend and backend (no shared package)
No auth layer; app is fully public
trade_offs.benefit: No session/JWT infrastructure needed for local tool

components.name: API Route Handlers
components.responsibility: Handle HTTP requests, parse bodies, call service layer, return responses

key_files.description: POST handler returning SSE stream for Claude generation
POST handler for iterative site refinement
Serves generated static files from output dir

components.name: Application Service Layer
components.responsibility: Orchestrate AI generation, GitHub fetching, prompt construction

key_interfaces.name: streamClaudeGeneration
key_interfaces.description: Streams Claude tool-use generation as SSE events

key_interfaces.name: getRepoContent
key_interfaces.description: Fetches GitHub repo metadata via Octokit

key_files.description: Core AI generation orchestration and streaming

components.name: Infrastructure Layer
components.responsibility: SQLite singleton, schema init, preset seeding

key_files.description: DB singleton with schema creation and preset seeding

components.name: Feature Components
components.responsibility: Stateful feature containers managing distinct UI domains

key_files.description: Prompt input, triggers generation, hosts ClaudeTerminal
Real-time SSE stream renderer with tool-use display
Chat UI for iterative post-generation refinement
GitHub repo URL input and metadata display
API key and provider configuration modal

components.name: Pages/Views Layer
components.responsibility: Root layout, metadata, global styles, main page composition

key_files.description: Root page composing all feature panels, manages session state
Root layout with metadata, Inter font, Header

contracts.description: { id, url, title, description, thumbnail, created_at }
Key-value pairs: ai_provider, gemini_api_key, github_token, github_repo

patterns.name: Server-Sent Events (SSE) Streaming
patterns.when_to_use: Long-running AI generation producing real-time progress
patterns.how_it_works: API route returns ReadableStream with text/event-stream; frontend reads via fetch + TextDecoder line buffering

patterns.name: Direct REST fetch
patterns.when_to_use: CRUD operations on pins, settings, presets, GitHub fetch
patterns.how_it_works: Frontend calls fetch() with async/await; manages loading/error state in useState

patterns.name: In-process function calls
patterns.how_it_works: TypeScript imports; no HTTP overhead; synchronous for SQLite, async for AI APIs

patterns.name: Callback prop drilling
patterns.when_to_use: Child-to-parent result propagation in React tree
patterns.how_it_works: Parent passes callback as prop; child invokes on completion

patterns.name: sessionStorage persistence
patterns.how_it_works: useEffect writes state to sessionStorage; useState initializer reads it back

integrations.purpose: Streaming landing page code generation with tool-use
Alternative AI provider for generation and refinement
Fetch repo README, metadata, file tree for generation context
Headless browser screenshot capture of generated pages

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Need real-time AI generation progress in UI
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Unidirectional stream; works on Vercel; no WebSocket server required

pattern_selection_guide.pattern: Direct REST fetch with useState loading/error
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: No caching library needed; data is small and user-initiated

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Backend service accessing database or calling AI
pattern_selection_guide.pattern: In-process function call to src/lib/* modules
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Same Node.js process; avoids HTTP overhead; TypeScript type safety

pattern_selection.streaming_ai_response: Return ReadableStream from API route with text/event-stream header
pattern_selection.database_access: Import getDb() from src/lib/db.ts; use prepared statements
pattern_selection.multi_provider_ai: Check settings.ai_provider then branch to Claude or Gemini path

error_mapping.description: Settings table missing gemini_api_key or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
Octokit throws on invalid owner/repo slug
data/ directory not writable; check process.cwd() permissions

stack.name: Next.js
stack.purpose: Full-stack framework: API routes + React SSR/CSR

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.purpose: Static typing across all source files

stack.name: Tailwind CSS
stack.purpose: Utility-first CSS with CSS variable theming

stack.name: better-sqlite3
stack.purpose: Synchronous SQLite for pins, settings, presets

stack.name: @anthropic-ai/claude-code
stack.purpose: Claude streaming code generation with tool-use

stack.name: @google/generative-ai
stack.purpose: Gemini API for generation and refinement fallback

stack.name: @octokit/rest
stack.purpose: GitHub API client for repo metadata extraction

templates.description: Standard Next.js API route with DB access
Client component with fetch and loading state

frontend.rendering_strategy: Hybrid: SSR for root layout/metadata, CSR ('use client') for all interactive feature components

ui_components.name: Home
ui_components.description: Root page; manages siteDir, sessionId, provider state; composes all panels

ui_components.name: ClaudeTerminal
ui_components.description: Terminal UI consuming SSE stream; renders tool-use events, file ops, costs

ui_components.name: GeneratePanel
ui_components.description: Prompt input; triggers generation stream; conditionally renders ClaudeTerminal

ui_components.name: RefinementChat
ui_components.description: Chat UI posting to /api/generate/refine with sessionId continuity

ui_components.name: PinBoard
ui_components.description: Masonry grid of pins from /api/pins; hosts AddPinModal

ui_components.name: PreviewFrame
ui_components.description: Iframe pointing to /api/preview/[...path] for generated site preview

ui_components.name: Header
ui_components.description: App header with logo and settings button; conditionally renders SettingsPanel

state_management.approach: React hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef) + sessionStorage for cross-refresh persistence
state_management.server_state: Direct fetch() calls per component; no caching library (no SWR/React Query)
state_management.local_state: Each feature component owns its loading/error/data state; root page.tsx owns siteDir/sessionId/provider
state_management.rationale: Linear data flow and single-user local tool make global state management unnecessary

data_fetching.name: Direct JSON fetch
data_fetching.mechanism: fetch() with async/await; useState for loading/error/data
data_fetching.when_to_use: CRUD operations: pins, settings, presets, GitHub

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes with CSS custom properties (--surface, --text, --text-secondary, --text-muted, --bg-elevated, --radius-lg) for theming. Dark theme applied at html element level in layout.tsx.
key_conventions: All interactive components use 'use client' directive
CSS variables referenced as bg-[var(--surface)] Tailwind arbitrary values
sessionStorage key: pinlaunch_session for state persistence
SSE events parsed with line-split buffer strategy in ClaudeTerminal

developer_recipes.task: Add a new API endpoint with database access
steps: 1. Create src/app/api/[feature]/route.ts with export async function GET/POST
2. Import getDb from '@/lib/db' and run prepared statements
3. Return Response.json(result) or error with appropriate status code

developer_recipes.task: Add a new React feature panel
steps: 1. Create src/components/[Name]Panel.tsx with 'use client' directive and useState hooks
2. Fetch data via fetch('/api/[feature]') in useEffect or event handlers
3. Import and render the component in src/app/page.tsx alongside existing panels

developer_recipes.task: Add a new SSE streaming endpoint
steps: 1. Create a ReadableStream factory function in src/lib/generate.ts emitting JSON lines
2. Create route.ts returning new Response(stream, { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream' } })
3. Consume in frontend with fetch + ReadableStream reader + TextDecoder line buffer pattern matching ClaudeTerminal.tsx

developer_recipes.task: Add a new AI generation preset
steps: 1. Add INSERT into seedPresets() in src/lib/db.ts (or run SQL manually on existing db)
3. Confirm PresetsPanel.tsx renders it (no code change needed if using generic list render)

architecture_diagram:
graph TD
    Browser[Browser] --> Home[page.tsx Root]
    Home --> GenPanel[GeneratePanel]
    Home --> PinBoard[PinBoard]
    Home --> Preview[PreviewFrame]
    Home --> Refine[RefinementChat]
    GenPanel --> Terminal[ClaudeTerminal]
    Terminal -->|SSE fetch| StreamAPI[/api/generate/stream]
    Refine -->|POST| RefineAPI[/api/generate/refine]
    PinBoard -->|GET/POST| PinsAPI[/api/pins]
    Preview -->|GET| PreviewAPI[/api/preview/...path]
    StreamAPI --> GenLib[src/lib/generate.ts]
    RefineAPI --> GenLib
    GenLib -->|tool-use| Claude[Anthropic Claude]
    GenLib -->|fallback| Gemini[Google Gemini]
    GenLib -->|writes files| FS[Output Filesystem]
    StreamAPI --> DB[(SQLite db.ts)]
    PinsAPI --> DB

pitfalls.description: data/builder.db is written to process.cwd() which is ephemeral on Vercel; all pins/settings/presets reset on redeploy
pitfalls.recommendation: Run locally or mount a persistent volume; consider migrating to Turso/LibSQL for production

pitfalls.description: TCP fragmentation can split a JSON event across multiple read() chunks; missing the buffer = lines.pop() pattern will cause JSON.parse errors
pitfalls.recommendation: Always use the split('\n') + pop() remainder buffer pattern as in ClaudeTerminal.tsx; validated in src/lib/stream-parsing.test.ts

pitfalls.description: better-sqlite3 is synchronous and native; it cannot be used in Edge Runtime or client components; will crash if imported in 'use client' files
pitfalls.recommendation: Never import src/lib/db.ts in components; always access via fetch to /api/* routes

pitfalls.description: seedPresets() only runs once on first DB connection (checked via SELECT COUNT); manually deleted presets will not be re-seeded without dropping the table
pitfalls.recommendation: To reset presets, DELETE FROM presets then restart the server to trigger re-seed

pitfalls.area: Dual AI provider stream format mismatch
pitfalls.description: Claude uses tool_use event types; Gemini uses blocking response; ClaudeTerminal SSE parser only handles Claude event schema
pitfalls.recommendation: Refine endpoint handles Gemini separately; do not route Gemini through the ClaudeTerminal component

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: streamClaudeGeneration() in src/lib/generate.ts creates a ReadableStream emitting JSON-formatted SSE events for Claude tool-use calls. Frontend ClaudeTerminal reads via fetch + TextDecoder line buffering, dispatching each event to UI state handlers.
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: const res = await fetch('/api/generate/stream', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ userPrompt }) });
tips: Keep SSE event payloads under 64KB to avoid TCP fragmentation issues
Maintain sync between ClaudeTerminal.tsx handleEvent() and src/lib/stream-parsing.test.ts when adding event types
Always set Cache-Control: no-cache and Connection: keep-alive headers on SSE responses

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: getDb() in src/lib/db.ts returns a WAL-mode SQLite singleton. Auto-creates schema and seeds presets on first connection. All API routes use this single accessor.
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: const db = getDb(); const pins = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM pins ORDER BY created_at DESC').all();
tips: Only call getDb() in API routes (Node.js runtime); never in client components
WAL files (db-wal, db-shm) must be on same filesystem as main db file
data/ directory auto-created via mkdirSync; ensure process.cwd() is writable

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: getRepoContent() in src/lib/github.ts uses Octokit to fetch README, package.json, file tree, and repo metadata via parallel Promise.all calls. Results feed into AI generation prompt.
tips: Public API rate limit is 60 req/hr; provide github_token from settings for 5000 req/hr
README and package.json returned as base64; decode before use
Recursive tree listing includes all files; filter to avoid prompt size bloat

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: settings.ai_provider value ('claude' or 'gemini') read from SQLite at request time in refine and generate routes. Claude path uses streaming tool-use; Gemini path uses blocking generateContent call.
tips: Test both provider paths independently; event schemas differ significantly
ClaudeTerminal component only handles Claude SSE event types
Provider selection persisted to sessionStorage via page.tsx for UI consistency

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Generated HTML/CSS/JS files written to process.cwd()/output/{dirName}/ by generate.ts. The /api/preview/[...path] catchall route reads files from this directory and returns them with correct MIME types and directory traversal protection.
tips: Directory traversal protection must check resolved path stays within output/ directory
MIME types inferred from file extension; ensure .css and .js extensions used in generation
PreviewFrame uses refreshTrigger prop to force iframe reload after refinement

development_rules.rule: Always initialize SQLite with WAL pragma via getDb() singleton; never create direct Database instances in route files
Always export HTTP verb functions (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) from route.ts files; never use default exports for API routes
Always mirror ClaudeTerminal.tsx SSE event handler logic in src/lib/stream-parsing.test.ts when adding new event types
development_rules.source: src/lib/stream-parsing.test.ts — duplicates handleEvent, handleToolUse, TOOL_ICONS from ClaudeTerminal

development_rules.rule: Always run tests with vitest run (not vitest watch) for non-interactive CI execution
Always use npm; no yarn or pnpm — project uses package-lock.json exclusively
development_rules.source: package-lock.json lockfileVersion 3 present; no yarn.lock or pnpm-lock.yaml

development_rules.rule: Always add 'use client' directive to components using useState/useEffect; omit for server-only layout/page files
development_rules.source: src/components/GeneratePanel.tsx, ClaudeTerminal.tsx — all interactive components are client components

deployment.runtime_environment: Vercel (inferred from Next.js 15 + no Docker/cloud config; local dev also supported)
compute_services: Vercel Serverless Functions for src/app/api/* routes
Vercel Edge Network for static assets
deployment.serverless_functions: All src/app/api/**/route.ts files auto-deployed as Vercel Serverless Functions
distribution: Local via npm run dev / start.sh
supporting_services: SQLite local file (data/builder.db) — ephemeral on Vercel
GitHub API (public, optional token for higher rate limits)
Google Gemini API (gemini_api_key in settings table)
deployment.environment_config: .env.local (Next.js convention; inferred from API key usage patterns in generate.ts and route handlers)
